mappings.value: r times e to the i psi equals the integral from minus pi to pi of e to the i theta times one over N times the sum of Dirac delta spikes at the oscillator phases theta sub j, integrated in theta. The delta-comb factor is the empirical phase density of the finite population on the circle.

Equation 4 rewrites the finite-N order parameter as an integral against an empirical density. Nothing continuous has happened yet; the density is still just a sum of delta spikes at the actual oscillator phases.

This is the bridge from the discrete average in Eq. 2 to the continuum density language that appears later in Eq. 5 and Eq. 7. It shows how sums over oscillators can be re-expressed as integrals over phase.
Use Eq. 4 when the point is to pass from a finite collection of oscillator phases to a density-style description. It is the conceptual precursor to the continuum order-parameter formula in Eq. 5.
